title: rawhide: podman run /no/such/command : hangs
body:
The two special-case 126 and 127 commands are now hanging:
```
# podman run alpine /no/such/command
[hangs forever]
# podman run alpine /etc
[same]
```
Interestingly, adding `--log-level=debug` fixes the problem.

The timing (of when this started happening), and the log-level thing, makes me suspect that it's related to #4124 

Rawhide only; cannot reproduce on f30 with runc.

comments.body: I reported this Friday morning on IRC

I can reproduce this with `podman-1.6.0-0.2.rc2.git9181c65.fc31.x86_64` and `crun-0.9.1-1.fc31.x86_64` on Fedora 31:
```
$ podman run registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:30 fubar
<stuck>
```

Correct. I haven't tried podman-remote. This is with root.

Just tried rootless, and *all* podman-run commands hang.
F30, crun and runc both, things work fine. Has to be v2 related.
Per discussion in IRC, axe my "v2" comment, it seems like a kernel issue

I don't think the kernel issue is causing this issue, I am not able to reproduce it yet.

The kernel issue seems a new one happening on Rawhide that prevents Podman to work at all: https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=1757104
...and the diff between those two commits is documentation-only. There is obviously something else going on.

Possibly important difference between the last successful run and first failing one:
```
works: conmon-2.0.0-2.fc32.x86_64
fails: conmon-2.0.2-0.1.dev.git422ce21.fc32.x86_64
```
My VM is hosed, will try to build a new one and downgrade conmon.

It's conmon; or at least the podman-conmon combination:
```
podman \ conmon             |   2.0.0-2  |  2.0.2-0.1.dev.git422ce21
----------------------------+------------+--------------------------
1.5.2-0.59.dev.git7312811   | [untested] | pass
1.6.0-0.36.dev.git01b7af8   | pass       | *HANG*
```
IOW: downgrading conmon resolves the hang.

Duh, my bad: the reason it passed with podman-1.5.2 is that conmon is bundled. After removing `/usr/libexec/podman/conmon`, results are the same as with podman-1.6: hangs with conmon-2.0.2, ok with conmon-2.0.0

problem commit it: https://github.com/containers/conmon/pull/65
@giuseppe can you PTAL? I tried taking a stab at a fix, we are stuck on the waitpid... I guess the cleanup process is hanging?

>  @giuseppe can you PTAL? I tried taking a stab at a fix, we are stuck on the waitpid... I guess the cleanup process is hanging?

I am still struggling trying to reproduce it, reinstalling F31 right now.  I think the waitpid can hang only if the cleanup process doesn't terminate.  If you can easily reproduce the issue, do you see any other podman processes (beside the pause process) lying around?

@giuseppe I wasn't able to reproduce either. @mheon gave me access to a VM that could yesterday, and I couldn't see any other running process in `ps aux | grep conmon` which was especially odd. It seemed like the cleanup process did finish but waitpid didn't terminate...
Concur, this was fixed in crun
